design_philosophy.core_loop: Place bombs to clear walls and defeat enemies, navigate maze-like levels, reach exit door. Procedurally generated layouts with strategic bomb placement timing.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing bomb cooldown and blast radius while avoiding enemy AI patterns in confined spaces. Risk-reward of destroying walls vs. creating escape routes.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Strategic demolition expert navigating claustrophobic mazes with controlled explosions.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasizes puzzle-solving and spatial planning over action reflexes. Randomized level generation combined with fixed difficulty progression. Hidden bonus conditions reward mastery and creative play (chain reactions, specific enemy avoidance patterns). Game expects players to discover obscure unlock mechanics organically.

difficulty.lives_system: Continue-based; player given multiple attempts per stage
difficulty.continues_system: Unlimited continues with stage-based progression checkpoint system
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing. 50 stages total with consistent difficulty scaling. Players progress linearly through stages.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Low agency. Difficulty predetermined by stage number and procedural level layout. Hidden bonus challenges provide optional mastery goals but do not affect progression.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear stage progression (1-50). Hidden bonus items unlock through specific stage conditions (no walls destroyed, chain reactions, timing challenges). Obscure conditions require discovery rather than traditional unlock trees.
replayability_hooks: Procedurally generated level layouts within each stage
Hidden bonus items with 8.5-second acquisition window create speedrun/puzzle hunting incentives
Nakamoto-san bonus requires perfectionist no-wall-destruction run
Dezeniman-san bonus requires destructive completionist with chain-reaction mastery
Goddess Mask bonus rewards perimeter-circling strategic play
Bonus Target rewards speed-rushing and optimal pathing
Cola Bottle bonus rewards sustained movement and timing discipline

world_building_techniques: Bonus item cameos reference Hudson Soft's other franchises (Star Force, Dezeni World)
Programmer portrait (Nakamoto-san) as unlockable easter egg
Lode Runner sequel reference in ending text (regional variations obscure this in NA release)
Prequel relationship to Lode Runner implied through ending

danger_signals: Game difficulty escalates across 50-stage linear progression; later stages represent dangerous mastery checkpoints
Procedural level generation prevents pattern memorization; each playthrough presents novel spatial threats
Enemy AI patterns vary stage-by-stage creating escalating complexity
environmental_storytelling: Maze layouts with destructible walls create narrative of confinement requiring tactical demolition
Procedural generation coupled with fixed stage difficulty suggests systemic institutional control
Hidden doors and items embedded within destructible environment reinforce exploration-based discovery culture
